key_points: Gender division creates unequal opportunities for women in education, jobs and politics
Feminist movements demand equal rights and political representation
Communalism occurs when religion becomes the main basis of political identity
Secularism protects freedom of religion in a democratic society
The caste system traditionally divides society into hierarchical groups
examples: Women having lower political representation shows gender inequality
Women's reservation debates are related to feminist demands
Political parties appealing to religious identity is communal politics
The Constitution allowing religious freedom reflects secularism
Reservation policies aim to improve representation of disadvantaged caste groups
formulas: Social equality = Equal rights + Equal opportunities + Absence of discrimination
Inclusive democracy = Participation of all social groups
exam_tips: Explain causes and effects of gender inequality in answers
Use constitutional examples when discussing secularism
Mention social reforms while writing about caste system
Include both positive and negative aspects of caste politics
